## reference · Retrieval-Augmented Generation
* Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) is a technique that enables large language models (LLMs) to retrieve and incorporate new information from external data sources.
* RAG improves LLMs by incorporating information retrieval before generating responses.
* RAG reduces AI hallucinations by pulling relevant text from databases, uploaded documents, or web sources.
* RAG reduces the need to retrain LLMs with new data, saving computational and financial costs.
* RAG allows LLMs to include sources in their responses, providing transparency and verifiability.

## RAG Process and Limitations
* LLMs can provide incorrect information if they rely solely on static training data.
* RAG does not solve all problems, as LLMs can still generate misinformation if they misinterpret context.
* LLMs with RAG prioritize new information through "prompt stuffing," which adds relevant context to the input.

## Applications and Benefits
* RAG enables LLM-based chatbots to access internal company data or generate responses based on authoritative sources.
* RAG helps LLMs stick to facts and reduces errors.

Sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Retrieval-augmented_generation
